Raw input for Energy security. Lower net energy import dependency is interpreted as greater energy security. Negative raw values can occur for net exporters and are not clipped.
Raw input for Debt capacity. Lower public debt/GDP is interpreted as greater debt capacity.
Raw input for R&D intensity. Higher R&D expenditure as a percentage of GDP is interpreted as stronger innovation capacity.
Raw input for Human capital. Higher adult tertiary educational attainment, age 25–64, is interpreted as stronger human-capital stock.
Raw input for Employment strength. Lower unemployment is interpreted as greater labour-market strength.
